verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Now at this time Damasus who had succeeded Liberius then presided over the church at Rome.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Cyril was still in possession of that at Jerusalem.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: The Antiochian church, as we have stated, was divided into three parts: for the Arians had chosen Dorotheus as the successor of their bishop Euzoïus; while one portion of the rest was under the government of Paulinus, and the others ranged themselves with Melitius, who had been recalled from exile.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: Lucius, although absent, having been compelled to leave Alexandria, yet maintained the episcopal authority among the Arians of that city; the Homoousians there being headed by Timothy, who succeeded Peter.

verses.book: The Ecclesiastical History of Socrates Scholasticus.
verses.text: At Constantinople Demophilus the successor of Eudoxius presided over the Arian faction, and was in possession of the churches; but those who were averse to communion with him held their assemblies apart.
